title: 第 34 節
en: which Agni opens when he brings light and wealth to his worshippers. Generally, however, they appear in the plural as the doors of wealth (I, 68, 5), as the doors (dvarau) of jRitsi, also as the doors of darkness (III, 5, 1). These doors, called by various names, are the eternal heavenly gates through which the representatives of the light of day come and go. What was on this side of the heavenly gates, was the home of man, what was on the other side, was the home of the gods. They assumed afterwards a kind of mythological personality, and under the name of Devir Dvara/t, the heavenly gates, we saw that they had a definite place assigned to them in the Apri hymns. Thus we read again and again (V, 5,5): ' Heavenly Gates move asunder, easy of access, for our protection, fill the sacrifice more and more.'

If then, phonetically, there is no possible objection to the derivation of 'AttoWcov from a Sanskrit form ""Apa-var-yan, or *Apa-val-yan 1, and if the opening of the heavenly gates was a characteristic feature of several of the solar or luminous deities in the Veda, the question arises whether in the myths about Apollon also some traces of this character of door-opener or porter of the sky may be discovered. We have mentioned already some of the epithets of Apollon, clearly referring to the opening of gates, such as that of 'A7roXXwv Ovpcuos 2. This is Apollon of the door, a name which, like Propylaios, was in later times explained as he whose statue stood at the door, but which long before the invention of statues had a very different meaning, as opening the gate of 1 The Cypric form 'AnetXav stands for 'A7reAya)i> = Apavaryan.
2 Gerhard, Griech. Mythologie, § 308, 6.

Vi] APOLLON. 693 the sky, and as stepping forth from the door of the morning, as 'E/c/3acrios and 'Eju/3acrio9. Besides having the epithet of 'Ecpos, he is actually called 'A/3e'Xio?, which was the Kretan name of Helios, the sun, and has sometimes been considered as the true etymon of Helios. Artemis, his sister, born with him at Delos, is likewise called Propylaia. A nymph who by Apollon became the mother of Kyknos, was called Thyria, while another, called Thero, bore him a son called Chairon. These names are generally explained as derived from localities where Apollon was worshipped, but the question is whether here, as in other cases, the localities were not originally called after Apollon and his friends. Thyrion was the place of a temple of Apollon near Chaironeia l, and in Italy Thurium or Thurii, the modern Sybaris, was famous as a place of worship of Apollon. It is quite possible, therefore, though I shall say no more, that the nymphs who were united with Apollon received their names originally for the same reason for which he himself had been called Thyraios.

I write all this being well aware of the objections which have been raised against explaining the name of Apollon or Apellon as meaning originally the opener of the heavenly gates. Greek scholars, however, should not forget that we actually have in the Aiolic dialect a7re'XXa> in the sense of a7ro/cXetaj, to shut off, and that the act of shutting off the darkness implies the act of bringing out the light. If mathematical proof is required of 'AneXXcov having been Apavaryan (Abwehrer), I confess I cannot produce it. It is easy to say that a mere opener, a janitore, or even a herald of the day could never 1 Gerhard, 1. c, p. 321.
694 APOLLON. [chap.

have grown into the glorious personality of the Greek Apollon. But we must not be so positive in our negative criticism. Just as the most perfect Greek statues were the last outcome of a movement that began with crude stones and hideous idols, just as the Hermes of Praxiteles is the direct descendant of the old images of the god which consisted of two stones on each side and a third placed across \ the mythological and religious conceptions also of Aeschylus and Sophocles presuppose a continuous growth beginning with the simplest and crudest conceptions of the agents or powers of nature. Far from looking on the Vedic hymns or on the Homeric poems as representing the primeval beginnings of mythological and religious thought among the Aryas, I have pointed out again and again that we can perceive rings within rings in their language, rings within rings in their mythology, and that the antecedents which we have to postulate require to be measured by geological rather than by historical periods. We may find it difficult to understand why a god of light, of wisdom, of prophecy, and of healing should originally have been called the Opener, or Revealer, *Apavaryan or Apollon ; why his enemies, the powers of darkness, whether of the dark night or of the dark clouds, should have been called Coverers (VWtra), and why at last this vritra should come to mean enemy in general, so that vWtraghna, lit. VWtra- killer, became the genius of victory, and the later Persian Bahram, the name of the fire that is kept burning to the present day to secure protection and victory to its worshippers.
1 Casanowics, Relig. Ceremonies in the Talmud, Proceedings of Am. Orient. Society, March, 1894, p. lxxix.

Vl] APOLLON. 695 If Vritra, from var, was the coverer or the genius of darkness, Apa-vWtra, Apa-vrmvan, or Apa-varyan would have been the most appropriate names of the uncoverer or the genius of light, one of those fundamental conceptions which was personified in Agni, in Indra, and in ever so many Aryan gods.

This might suffice to show that the name of Apollon or Apellon was originally meant for the god who shuts off the darkness of the night or the darkness of the clouds, — both, as we saw, constantly running together, — and who thus opens or reveals the light. In matters like these we must learn to be satisfied with what is possible or probable, we cannot, nay we ought not to clamour for mathematical certainty.

As I derive Apollon not from apa + ar, or 0,770 a Au/u, but from apa-var, it might be objected that the Greek form op or ep would have had an initial Digamma, and this ought to have asserted its former presence by preventing contraction with the final vowel of the preposition. Thus we have arroepyo), aTToeiKOi, airoenreiv, &c. But by the side of airoenreiv we find airenrelv in classical Greek, so that *airo\\eiv for *airo-o\\eiv would rest on very strong analogy. There is besides the Lesbian aireWm in which eXXco, Dor. Frfkb), Horn. et\o>, certainly begins with the Digamma, and in which the final o of the preposition is omitted just as it is in 'AireWcov, the Doric form of AttoWojv 1.
If, then, etymology teaches us that Apollon was in the beginning conceived as a revealer of light, if his epithets teach us that his original home was in 1 Brugmann, Grundriss, § 611.

Delos, or in the East, that he was Phoibos, brilliant, having for his sister Phoibe, the moon, that he was Lykeios, illuminating, gold-haired, and with blonde locks, is it not clear that nearly all the stories told of him confirm his original character or at all events are never in conflict with it ? We saw already that the name of his mother, Leto, meant the night or, it may be, the earth during the darkness of the night. It is quite true that Leto cannot be derived directly from Xadelv, but that does not prevent us from taking \adeiv and latere as parallel roots. A comparison of Leto with Rati for Ratri, night, is tempting, but hardly necessary. Delos like Asteria meant originally the bright place where Heaven and Earth seemed to meet, and whence sprang the golden light of the young sun, who was hence called Delios, the brilliant. If one myth tells us that Leto was in travail for nine days, that may refer to the vernal sun who, before beginning his northward course, was supposed, at the summer solstice, to remain stationary for nine or even twelve days.

In that case Apollon, though the daily sun, would have been looked upon as beginning his victorious vernal career at the time of the Northern solstice. In that case even the preceding travels of Leto all over the earth might find an explanation. Her laying hold of a palm-tree reminds one of similar cases, particularly that of Buddha's mother, Maya \ Ilithyia or Eileithyia. As to Ilithyia {ElKeid via), who was kept away by Here, but brought at last by Iris to assist at the 1 It seems to me impossible to see in this act an allusion to the fire-sticks, as v. Schroeder suggests.

Vl] ILITHYIA. 697 birth of Apollon, her name is difficult to explain. It must be remembered that she, like the Dawn in the Veda, was originally represented as more than one, the Ilithyias, but that, after the time of Homer, she is spoken of as one goddess, and not only as assisting others at childbirth, but as herself giving birth to a child. She was called Auge (Avyr) iv yovacrc), and as Auge was a name of the dawn, it was supposed that she received that name because she presided over the dawn of life. In Latin too she is called Lucina or Juno Lucina, which points to light as her original character. If she was called older than Kronos, that again was interpreted as implying that generation began at the beginning of all things. Sometimes Here, who watches over marriage (ya/r/puo?), and who is called the mother of Ilithyia, is identified with her daughter, nay Artemis and Diana even (possibly, like Lucina, representing the moon) are supposed to have performed her functions.

The name is given as ElXeiOvca, ion. ElXtfOvLa, 'EXevOvia, and also 'EXevdco, and it seems to me that Eleutho, instead of being an abbreviation, is possibly the simple parallel form of Eleuthyia and Eileithyia. She would then have been called the comer, or, if we take into account the Kretan iXevdco, to bring, the Bringer, a meaning that would be welcomed by all who see in Fors primigenia the Bringer of good things. Krete was the home of Eleutho, she herself was born there in the Amnisian cave. The same root iXvd (ruh) might supply a key to the name of 'HXvctlov, that which is to come, l'avenir, as the name of the Elysian fields in the West, the home of the blessed.
We now return to Apollon, who after the advent of the coming goddess Eileithyia l was born at Delos, i. e. in the East, also called Ortygia (vartika) for reasons explained before, and Asteria.

Apollon's early love for Daphne also has long been accounted for as referring to the Dawn. That his fight with Python (Ahir budhnya) should be his first heroic achievement, may possibly be explained by the fact that at his first appearance above the horizon he had only just escaped from the powers of the lower regions (budhna), the serpents who were killed by the first rays or arrows of the young god. It is known, however, that other authorities defer this fight against Python to a later time and to a different locality. This shows the freedom with which mythology was handled in its origin, and shows how fully justified we are in claiming a similar freedom in our analysis of mythology. That the original idea of the fight of Apollon against Python was that of the struggle of the light of the morning against the darkness below the horizon or of the vanishing night admits of little doubt. But it is perfectly conceivable that when Apollon's sanctuary was established at Delphi, the same myth should have been localised there. Prof. Forchhammer, who had explored the neighbourhood of Delphi most carefully, gives us an explanation of the battle between Apollon and Python, which deserves more attention than it has hitherto received. I quite follow the opinion expressed by M. Decharme in his valuable Mythologie de la Grece, p. 1 00.
1 Qvia, if it occurs at the end of other mythological names, would seem to mean wind, cf. OveWa, so that 'Opeidvia would be the mountain wind.

Vl] APOLLON. 699 'M. Forchhammer,' he writes, 'a Danish scholar (no, German), after having carefully explored the nature of the soil at Delphi, takes the dragon Python as the symbol of the torrent which at the beginning of spring descends from the slopes of Parnassus, forms a waterfall between the two rocks, Nauplia and Hyampeia, bounds along the successive terraces of the Delphic amphitheatre, and precipitates itself into the valley of the Pleistos. This torrent, swollen by the rains of winter and the melting of the snow on the hills, as it carries along and destroys everything in its impetuous fury, moving along tortuously like a serpent, might well have been compared by the people of Delphi to a formidable dragon, devastating the country and terrifying the people and their flocks. But in summer the water flows off, falls and evaporates, and the hot rays of the sun dry the bed of the torrent. The monster has been pierced by the arrows of the god. His body begins to putrify and he receives henceforth the name of Python, that is, the putrid.'

I cannot say that I feel quite convinced, but that these secondary myths spring up sometimes from a mere wish to explain unintelligible names, we saw in such cases as that of Argeiphontes, and if this explanation offered itself to Forchhammer while exploring that neighbourhood, it is quite possible that it may have occurred to the ancient Greeks also, when establishing the new and permanent sanctuary for Apollon at Delphi.

What is the meaning of the servitude imposed on Apollon under Admetos, I have shown before by a reference to the Mexican Inca who though a worshipper of the sun, nay worshipped himself as a descendant of the sun, declined to recognise the sun as the Supreme God on account of vol. 11. t 700 APOLLON. [CHAP.
The chains That bind thee to the path that God ordains That thou shouldst trace1.

This servitude has been painted in different ways, but the original thought is always the same. And as the solar god was represented as possessing herds of 350 oxen or sheep, it was natural that as a slave also he should be employed, as Apollon was by Laomedon, to watch his cattle.

Again when the annual travels of the sun had led him far away to the north, where he remained during the long winter, Apollon himself was supposed to enjoy among the Hyperboreans a happier time than the raw winter in Greece.

There can hardly be any doubt that the journey to and the stay of Apollon with the Hyperboreans reflect the annual movements of the sun from South to North, and from North to South. We have only to read Plutarch, who tells us that from spring to autumn Delphi resounds with hymns of praise and paeans to Apollon, while in winter the paean was silent and nothing was heard but the dithyrambos and songs on the sufferings of Dionysos, in order to understand the character of the Greek Hyperboreans and of Apollon's relation to them. The date of the return of Apollon from the Hyperboreans to Greece varies from spring to the middle of summer. His departure was the auohiqixia, his return the eViS^/xta. If, however, any doubt could still remain as to the solar origin of Apollon, his intimate relations with the Charites would settle it, as in the case of Hermes. Sometimes these Charites are called the daughters of Helios and Aigle, names which speak for them- 1 Chips, iv, p. 122.

Vl] APOLLON. 701 selves ; nor are their own names, Aglaia, Euphrosyne, Thalia, Pasiphae, Kleta, or Phaenna, less significant. The epithet KovpoTpocfyoi they share in common with Apollon. Apollon was represented as carrying the three Charites on his hand, which is the same idea as when the sun-god in the Veda is represented on his golden chariot drawn by his Haritas. They are called Lampos and Phaethon, or Pyroeis, Eoos, Aithon or Phlegon in Greece, names that are very outspoken, while in India these horses of the sun are simply called Haritas, i. e. the red or bright ones. Though Apollon was originally a light-giving, joygiving, and gracious god, we can easily understand that the more terrible aspects of the sun also should have been reflected in him. The sun helps vegetation, but the same sun may destroy the harvest, may send pestilence and kill the very people who worshipped him. In the Veda also the solar powers assume from time to time a terrible character ; and Apollon as the angry god, as moving along vvktL ioLKm, like unto night, as ovXlos, baneful, as anoXkcuv, supposed to mean destroyer, comes so near to the Vedic Rudra that Rudra rather than Agni in his character of Surya, has been looked upon as the prototype of Apollon \ And curious enough, as Apollon wTas conceived not only as sending pestilence and death, but likewise as the physician and healer of illness, not only as ovXlos, baneful, but also as ovXlos, healthful, Rudra, the terrible, also was supposed to be possessed of all medicines. Hence Asklepios, the physician of the gods, was fabled to be the son of Apollon, while the other healing god, 1 K. Z., xxix, 225. T 2 y02 APOLLON. [chap.

PaiSon or Paian (Uairjwv, Tlaudv), was actually identified with Apollon. That he became likewise the physician of the soul, the purifier and deliverer from sin (acDT-qp), these are characteristics which we must not hope to find in the Vedic gods, though they are not entirely absent, as we may see in the prayers for forgiveness of sin addressed to Varuna and others. Neither can we look in the Veda for a god representative of music and of prophecy as Apollon was in Greece. These are the reflexions of Greek life, and though they may have been assigned to Apollon without contravening his original physical character, they are of Greek, not of Aryan growth.

In all these respects, the case of Apollon allows us to see very clearly what we may and what we may not expect in a comparison of Vedic and Greek or any other Aryan mythology. There are some scholars who deny the very existence of a Pan- Aryan mythology. With them, after all that has been written, I suppose I need not argue. But these are the very writers who in the absence of all arguments try to throw discredit on mythological researches by pointing the finger of scorn at the want of agreement in the comparison of Aryan deities and in the interpretation of Aryan myths. It is well known that Kuhn has tried to identify Apollon with Rudra, von Schroeder with Agni, and I myself with neither of them, or rather with both. I have openly stated the case so as to give our adversaries a splendid opportunity. And yet to those who have followed me up to this point my answer cannot be doubtful. I hold that it is a fundamental mistake to expect coincidences in the names and achievements of Aryan gods after they have once entered into their national phase.

Vl] APOLLON. 703 If such coincidences occur, they are exceptions, and have to be accounted for, just as we have to find excuses if we identify the English ' to have ' with Latin ' habere.' When Aryan words have entered on their national development, when the common Aryan language has once become Latin or French, Gothic or English, we must not look for close resemblances between Gothic and Latin, English and French. We must remember first of all what is peculiarly Latin or peculiarly Gothic, and then strip the words of these later crusts before we try to discover their original form. If we come across such apparent coincidences as ' to call ' and Kakelv, ' to care ' and cura, we should know at once that they cannot be real, because English and Greek and English and Latin have no right to agree so minutely, or if they do, as in the case of ' to have ' and ' habere,' such exceptional agreements have to be very carefully justified.
zh: 阿耆尼在為其信仰者帶來光明與財富時所開啟之門。然而，一般而言，它們以複數形式出現，如財富之門（I, 68, 5），亦如吠陀之門（dvarau），以及黑暗之門（III, 5, 1）。這些門以各種名稱被稱呼，乃是永恆的天界之門，日光的代表藉由此門往來。在天界之門這一側者，乃人類的家園；在天界之門彼一側者，乃諸神的家園。它們後來獲得了某種神話人格，而在提婆‧德瓦拉（Deva Dvara），即天界之門的名號下，我們已見其在《阿普里讚歌》（Apri hymns）中被指定了明確的位置。因此我們一再讀到（V, 5, 5）：「天界之門移開，易於通行，為護衛我等，日益充盈祭祀。」

如果說在音韻學上，將 'Απόλλων 衍生自梵文形式 *Apa-var-yan 或 *Apa-val-yan¹ 並無任何可能的反對理由，而且打開天門確實是吠陀中數位太陽神或光明提婆的特徵性功能，那麼我們便應追問：在阿波羅（Apollon）的神話中，是否也能發現這位開門者或天空守護者的某些痕跡？我們已經提及阿波羅的若干上號，明確指涉開啟大門的功能，例如 'Απόλλων Θυραῖος²。這是「門之阿波羅」，這一名號與 Propylaios 相同，在後世被解釋為其雕像立於門前之人，然而在塑像藝術發明之遠古年代，它具有完全不同的含義──即開啟天之門戶者。 ¹ 塞浦路斯形式 'Απέλλαν 代表 'Απέλυων = Apavaryan。 ² 編注。
根據您提供的內容，這是一條學術註腳參考文獻，其翻譯如下： 2 格哈德，《希臘神話學》，§ 308, 6。

阿波羅。693 天空，以及作為從晨曦之門踏出而來，如 'Ἔκβάσιος 及 'Ἐμβάσιος。除了具有 'Ἔσπος 的稱號外，他實際上被稱為 'Ἀβέλλιος，這是克里特人對太陽神赫利俄斯（Helios）的稱呼，有時被認為是赫利俄斯的真正語源。他的妹妹阿爾特彌斯，與他在得羅斯（Delos）同時誕生，同樣被稱為原殿女神（Propylaia）。一位名叫蒂里亞（Thyria）的女仙因與阿波羅結合而成為庫克諾斯（Kyknos）的母親，另一位名叫蒂羅（Thero）的女仙為他生了一個名叫凱隆（Chairon）的兒子。這些名字通常被解釋為源自阿波羅受到崇拜的地點，但問題在於——就像其他情況一樣——這些地點最初是否並非以阿波羅及其同伴的名字命名。蒂里翁（Thyrion）是靠近凱隆尼亞（Chaironeia）之處阿波羅神廟的所在地，而在義大利，圖里姆或圖里（Thurium 或 Thurii），即現代的西巴里斯（Sybaris），曾作為阿波羅崇拜的著名地點而聞名。因此，雖然我不再多言，但完全有可能，與阿波羅結合的女仙最初因為與他被稱為蒂拉俄斯（Thyraios）相同的理由而獲得她們的名字。

我深知有人提出異議，反對將阿波羅（Apollon）或阿佩隆（Apellon）這個名字解釋為原本意指「天門的開啟者」。然而，希臘學者不應遺忘，我們確實在愛奧利方言中有 ἀπέλλα（apélla）一詞，其意義為 ἀποκλείω（apokleíō），即關閉；而關閉黑暗的行為本身就蘊含了驅出光明的行為。若要求對 Ἀπέλλων（Apollon）乃 Abwehrer（驅逐者）一詞提出數學上的證明，我坦言無法提出。人們容易說，一個單純的開啟者、門衛（janitore）或甚至白晝的傳令官絕不可能¹ ¹ Gerhard, 1. c, p. 321.
694 阿波羅。[章

已長成希臘阿波羅（Apollon）光輝燦爛的人格。但我們在進行否定性的批評時，不應過於武斷。正如最完美的希臘雕像乃是始於粗糙的石頭和醜陋偶像的運動之最後成果，正如普拉克西特列斯（Praxiteles）的赫爾墨斯雕像乃是古老神像的直接後裔——那些古老神像由兩塊石頭並排放置，再以第三塊橫放其上而成——神話學和宗教學的觀念亦然，埃斯庫羅斯（Aeschylus）和索福克勒斯（Sophocles）的神話和宗教觀念，同樣預設了一個連續的發展過程，始於對自然諸力或諸因素最簡單、最原始的觀念。我遠非將吠陀讚歌或荷馬詩篇視為雅利安人神話學和宗教思想之原始始源，反而一再指出，我們可以在其語言中發現層層疊疊的演變環節，在其神話中發現層層疊疊的演變環節，而且我們必須推定的先行要素需以地質學時期而非歷史時期來衡量。我們可能難以理解，何以一位光明、智慧、預言和醫療之神原本應被稱為「開啟者」或「啟示者」（*apavaryan* 或 *Apollon*）；為何其敵手——黑暗勢力，無論是黑夜之黑暗或烏雲之黑暗——應被稱為「遮蔽者」（*Vritra*）；為何最終這個 *vritra* 應演變成普遍意義的敵人，使得 *Vtraghna*（字面義為「毗栗多殺手」）成為勝利的精靈，而後來的波斯神名巴赫拉姆（*Bahram*）則是那時至今日仍被點燃以保護其信徒並為其祈福勝利的火的名字。
1 Casanowics, 《塔木德中的宗教儀式》，《美國東方學會會刊》，一八九四年三月，第七十九頁。
VI] 阿波羅。695 如果弗栗多（Vritra）源自「var」，乃為遮蔽者或黑暗之靈，則「阿帕‧弗栗多」（Apa-vṛitra）、「阿帕‧弗棼」（Apa-vṛṇvan）或「阿帕‧瓦耶」（Apa-varyan）便是開啟者或光明之靈最恰當的名稱，乃是那類根本觀念之一——此類觀念在阿耆尼（Agni）、因陀羅（Indra）及眾多雅利安諸神中得以人格化。
這足以表明，阿波羅（Apollon 或 Apellon）之名原本是指那位驅散夜晚黑暗或雲層黑暗的神祇——誠如我們所見，兩者不斷交融——並因而開啟或顯現光明的神。在諸如此類的事項上，我們必須學會滿足於可能或蓋然的解釋，我們不能，也不應當執意要求數學上的確定性。

我既然不是從 apa + ar 或 0,770 a Au/u 推導出 Apollon，而是從 apa-var 推導，可能有人會反駁說，希臘語形式 op 或 ep 本應具有初始雙字母音，而這應該藉由阻止與前置詞末尾元音的縮合來表明其先前存在。因此我們有 arroepyo)、aTToeiKOi、airoenreiv 等。但在 airoenreiv 之外，我們在古典希臘語中發現 airenrelv，所以 *airo\\eiv 作為 *airo-o\\eiv 的形式應該基於非常強大的類推。此外還有萊斯博方言的 aireWm，其中 eXXco（多立斯方言 Frfkb）、荷馬語 et\o> 確實以雙字母音開始，而其中前置詞的末尾 o 被省略，就如同在 'AireWcov（AttoWojv 的多立斯方言形式）中一樣。
倘若詞源學教導我們阿波羅（Apollon）在最初被構想為光的啟示者，倘若他的諸多別稱教導我們他的原始家鄉是<sup>1</sup> --- <sup>1</sup> Brugmann, Grundriss, § 611.

在得羅斯島（Delos），或在東方，他是斐波斯（Phoibos），光輝燦爛的神祇，其妹妹斐拜（Phoibe）乃月亮，他是呂凱俄斯（Lykeios），照耀大地者，金髮而金色鬈髮，難道不是清楚地表明，幾乎所有關於他的故事都證實了他的原初性質，或至少從未與之相衝突嗎？我們已經看到，他母親勒托（Leto）的名字意味著夜晚，或可能是指黑夜中的大地。雖然勒托確實不能直接從 Xadelv 推導出來，但這並不妨礙我們將 adeiv 和 latere 視為平行的語根。將勒托與梵文的「夜」（Rati 或 Ratri）相比較，令人心動，但幾乎沒有必要。得羅斯島如同阿斯特里亞（Asteria）一樣，最初意指天地相接的光明之處，年輕太陽的金色光芒由此湧現，故而被稱為德利俄斯（Delios），即光輝者。若一則神話告訴我們勒托陣痛長達九日，那可能指的是春日的太陽，在開始向北行進之前，據說在夏至時會保持靜止狀態長達九日，甚至十二日之久。

在那種情況下，儘管阿波羅是日間之日，卻應該被視為在北半球冬至時開始他勝利的春季生涯。在那種情況下，甚至連勒托先前遍歷全地的旅程也可能找到解釋。她抓住棕櫚樹的舉動令人想起類似的案例，特別是佛陀之母摩耶（Maya）的例子。至於伊利提亞（Ilithyia 或 Eileithyia，希臘文 Ἰλειθυία），她遭到赫拉阻攔，但最後被伊里絲帶來協助生產。¹ ¹ 我認為在這個行為中看到對鑽木取火的暗示是不可能的，如馮‧施羅德所建議的那樣。

伊利提亞的名字難以解釋，特別是在阿波羅的誕生方面。必須記住，她如同吠陀中的晨曦女神一樣，最初被表現為眾數形式（眾伊利提亞），但在荷馬時代之後，她被視為單一女神，不僅協助他人分娩，而且自己也生育了孩子。她被稱為奧革（Auge, Αὐγή），而奧革作為晨曦的名稱，人們推測她因掌管生命之晨而得此名。在拉丁文中，她也被稱為露西娜（Lucina）或朱諾‧露西娜（Juno Lucina），這指向光明乃是她最初的性質。若她被稱為比克洛諾斯更古老，那麼這又被解釋為暗示生育始於萬物之初。有時，監護婚姻（γαμήρυος）的赫拉——被稱為伊利提亞之母——被與其女兒認同，甚或阿爾特彌斯與戴安娜（或許如露西娜一樣，代表月亮）被認為曾執行過她的職能。

這個名字的記載形式為 ElXeiOvca、ElXtfOvLa、'EXevOvia，也作 'EXevdco，在我看來，Eleutho 與其說是一個縮略形式，不如說可能是 Eleuthyia 和 Eileithyia 的簡單對應形式。那麼她就被稱為「來臨者」，或者若考量到克里特方言 iXevdco（帶來）的含義，則是「帶來者」—— 這樣的含義必然為那些在 Fors primigenia 中看到「美物之帶來者」的人所歡迎。克里特是 Eleutho 的故鄉，她本身就誕生於 Amnisian 洞穴。同一個根源 iXvd（ruh）或許也能為 'HXvctlov 的名字提供鑰匙，該名字意為「將要到來的」、「l'avenir」（未來），即西方極樂世的別名，乃福祉之地。
我們現在回到阿波羅，他在女神伊萊提亞（Eileithyia）降臨之後，誕生於得洛斯島，即東方，此地亦稱為歐提基亞（vartika），其理由前已說明，另也稱為阿斯特里亞。

阿波羅對達芙妮的早期愛慕長久以來也被解釋為指涉曙光。他與巨蛇帕同（Ahir budhnya）的戰鬥應為其首次英雄業績，這個事實或許可以解釋為：在他首次出現於地平線上時，他才剛剛逃脫下界（budhna）的力量——那些被年輕之神的首批光線或箭矢所殺死的蛇類。然而，已知有其他權威著述將阿波羅與帕同的戰鬥推遲到後來的時代和不同的地點。這足以說明神話在其成因中被處理的自由度有多大，也充分證明了我們在分析神話時獲得類似自由的合理性。原初的阿波羅與帕同之戰的觀念乃是晨光與地平線下的黑暗或消逝之夜相搏鬥，這一點幾無疑問。但完全可以想見，當阿波羅的聖殿建立於德爾斐時，同一神話應當已在該地本地化。對德爾斐周邊地區進行最仔細考察的福克漢默教授為我們提供了一份關於阿波羅與帕同之戰的解釋，這份解釋應當受到比迄今為止所獲得的更多的關注。我完全贊同德夏姆先生在其寶貴著作《希臘神話學》（*Mythologie de la Grece*, p. 100）中所表達的觀點。
若 Qvia 出現在其他神話學名稱的末尾，似乎意謂風（cf. Ὀρεία），因此「Ὀρειδνία」應為山風。

第六卷] 阿波羅 699 『M‧福希哈默』，他寫道，『一位丹麥學者（不，是德國人），在仔細探究德爾斐土壤性質之後，將龍蛇皮同視為象徵——那條洪流在春初時節自帕那索斯山坡奔瀉而下，在兩塊岩石瑙普利亞與許姆佩亞之間形成瀑布，沿著德爾斐圓形劇場的逐級階梯奔騰，最後衝入普萊索斯谷地。這條溪流因冬季暴雨與山巔積雪融化而水勢洶洶，隨著其猛烈衝勢摧毀沿途一切，蜿蜒扭動如蛇行，德爾斐人民自然會將其比擬為一隻可怖的龍，摧殘家園、驚嚇人畜。然而到了夏季，水流消退、蒸發，烈日驕陽乾涸了溪床。怪獸已被眾神之神的箭矢刺穿。其軀體開始腐爛，自此得名為皮同（Python），也就是「腐爛之物」。』
我無法說自己完全信服，但這些次級神話（secondary myths）有時確實源於單純的解釋難解名字的願望，我們在阿爾蓋伊豐特斯（Argeiphontes）這類案例中看到了這一點。若此種解釋在福希哈默（Forchhammer）探索該地區時呈現於其心，那麼在古代希臘人為阿波隆在德爾斐建立新的永久聖殿時，同樣的解釋很可能也會浮現於他們的思想中。
我之前已經通過參考墨西哥印加人來說明強加在阿波羅（Apollon）身上、侍奉阿德墨托斯（Admetos）的奴役所具有的意義。這些印加人雖然是太陽的崇拜者，甚至將自己視為太陽的後裔而崇拜自己，但卻因故拒絕承認太陽為至高之神。
那些束縛你的鎖鏈，縛你於上帝所規定的路徑，那你應當追蹤的路徑¹。
這種奴役狀態以各種不同的方式被描繪，但其原始思想始終如一。既然太陽神被描述為擁有三百五十頭牛羊的畜群，那麼他作為奴隸也應當被雇用來看守牲畜，正如阿波羅（Apollon）曾被拉俄墨冬（Laomedon）所雇用一樣，這自然而然也就成立了。
同樣地，當太陽的週年運行將其帶往遙遠的北方，在漫長的冬季裡停留於彼處時，阿波羅本人據稱在極北之民（Hyperboreans）中享受著比希臘嚴酷冬季更為幸福的時光。

幾乎無可懷疑，阿波羅與極北之民的往來和停留反映了太陽從南向北、從北向南的年度運動。我們只需閱讀普魯塔克的記載，他告訴我們，從春季到秋季，德爾斐響徹著對阿波羅的讚美詩和酒神頌歌，而在冬季，酒神頌歌沉寂，人們聽到的只是狄奧尼索斯苦難的酒神頌（dithyrambos）與歌曲。由此足以理解希臘極北之民的性質及阿波羅與他們的關係。阿波羅從極北之民返回希臘的日期從春季變化至夏季中旬。他的出發是 *auohiqixia*，他的歸來是 *eViS^/xta*。然而，若對阿波羅的太陽起源仍有疑慮，他與美惠三女神（Charites）的密切關係可解決此事，正如赫爾墨斯的情況。有時這些美惠三女神被稱為太陽神赫利俄斯（Helios）與艾格麗（Aigle）的女兒，這些名字足以說明她們的性質。

自身；他們自有的名字——阿格萊亞（Aglaia）、歐芙羅絲涅（Euphrosyne）、塔利亞（Thalia）、帕西法厄（Pasiphae）、克萊塔（Kleta）或菲埃娜（Phaenna）——絲毫不遜其意義。KovpoTpocfyoi 這個稱號他們與阿波羅共享。阿波羅被描繪為在他的手掌上承載三位恩典女神，這與吠陀中日神被描繪為乘坐由其哈利塔斯（Haritas）駿馬駕駛的金色戰車相同。這些馬被稱為蘭普斯（Lampos）與法厄同（Phaethon），或皮羅艾斯（Pyroeis）、埃俄斯（Eoos）、艾同（Aithon）或弗勒貢（Phlegon）在希臘；這些名字含義昭然若揭，而在印度，這些日神之馬僅被稱為哈利塔斯（Haritas），即紅色或明亮的。儘管阿波羅最初是一位賦予光明、賦予喜樂與仁慈的神祇，我們不難理解太陽較為可怖的面向也應當在他身上有所反映。太陽助長植被生長，但同樣的太陽也可能摧毀收成，可能降下瘟疫，奪去敬拜他之人的生命。在吠陀中，太陽力量亦時而呈現恐怖的特性；阿波羅作為怒神，如夜行般移動（vktL ioLKm），如俄勒俄斯（ovXlos）那般有害，如阿波樂庫翁（anoXkcuv）那般據稱意為毀滅者，其與吠陀魯陀羅（Rudra）之近似程度足使魯陀羅——而非作為蘇利耶（Surya）的阿格尼——被視為阿波羅的原型¹。並且有趣的是，正如阿波羅被認為不僅發送瘟疫與死亡，而且亦是疾病的醫師與治癒者，不僅是俄勒俄斯（ovXlos）之有害者，而且也是俄勒俄斯（ovXlos）之健康者，魯陀羅這位恐怖之神亦被認為掌有諸般良藥。因此，諸神的醫師阿斯克勒庇俄斯（Asklepios）被傳說為阿波羅之子，而另一位治療之神 ¹ K. Z., xxix, 225.

帕伊松或帕伊安（Paian, Παιάν），實際上是與阿波羅（Apollon）同一的神祇。他後來成為靈魂的醫者、淨化者與救贖者（ἀποδύτης），這些特徵乃是我們不應期望在吠陀神祇中尋得的，雖然並未完全缺失，如我們在向伐樓那（Varuna）及其他諸神祈求赦罪的禱文中所見。我們同樣不能在吠陀中尋求一位代表音樂與預言的神祇，如阿波羅在希臘所具有的那般。這些乃是希臘生活的反映，雖然這些特性被賦予阿波羅而未與其原初的物質特徵相牴觸，但它們屬於希臘的產物，而非雅利安的成長。

在所有這些方面，阿波羅（Apollon）的案例使我們能夠非常清楚地看到，在吠陀與希臘或任何其他雅利安神話的比較中，我們可以期待什麼，以及不能期待什麼。有些學者否認泛雅利安神話的存在本身。對於他們，在已經撰寫的一切之後，我想我無須辯論。但這些正是那些在缺乏任何論據的情況下，試圖通過指責雅利安神祇的比較中缺乏一致性，以及對雅利安神話的詮釋中缺乏一致性，來詆毀神話學研究的作者。眾所周知，庫恩（Kuhn）試圖將阿波羅與魯陀羅（Rudra）相聯繫，馮‧施羅德（von Schroeder）與阿耆尼（Agni）相聯繫，而我本人則與兩者都無關，或者說是與兩者皆有關聯。我坦白地陳述了此案例，以便給予我們的對手一個極好的機會。然而，對於那些跟隨我到此為止的人，我的答案不會有疑問。我認為，期待雅利安諸神一旦進入他們的民族階段之後，在名稱和功績方面具有相同之處，這是一個根本性的錯誤。

關於阿波羅 703 如果這類巧合確實出現，它們乃是例外，必須加以說明——正如我們在將英文的「to have」與拉丁文的「habere」進行認同時，必須找出理由一樣。當雅利安語詞進入其民族發展階段，當共同的雅利安語言一旦演變成拉丁文或法文、哥德文或英文之時，我們就不應該期待哥德文與拉丁文、英文與法文之間有密切的相似之處。我們必須首先銘記何為特有的拉丁文特質或特有的哥德文特質，然後在試圖發現其原始形態之前，先將這些詞彙剝離後來的層殼。如果我們碰到諸如「to call」與 Kakelv、「to care」與 cura 這類明顯的巧合，應當立刻認識到它們不可能是真實的，因為英文與希臘文之間、英文與拉丁文之間無權在細節上如此精密地相符；或者，即使它們確實如「to have」與「habere」這類個案那樣相符，這種特殊的相符必須經過十分謹慎的論證才能成立。
